conflicts.description: Chapter 7 presents the East Undercroft as a chamber with a standing central anchor mass and a separate wall calibration table, while chapter 10 briefly describes the anchor as a broad disk set shallow in the floor.
conflicts.canonical_value: East Undercroft is a founder-era service chamber with a shoulder-high canted anchor stone at the center and floor rings and channels around it.
conflicts.resolution_note: Chapter 7 establishes the space first, and chapter 16's battle mechanics depend on a standing central mass that can be scrubbed, inscribed, bled onto, and braced under load. That version is more causally embedded than the chapter 10 shorthand.
